sections.text:
Subject to the provisions of section four, there shall be paid by everypassengeron every occasion on which he embarks on anaircraftother than adesignated airportat anyairportfor a destination outside Zambia, apassengerservicechargeat such rate as the Minister may by statutory instrument determine:
Provided that apassengerfor a destination outside Zambia who is a national or resident of Zambia may pay the equivalent of thechargein kwacha.
Thechargeat anairport, other than adesignated airport, shall be a debt due to the Government and shall be paid to thecollection agentprior to embarkation.
Subject to section four there shall be paid by everypassengerembarking on anaircraftat adesignated airportfor a destination outside or within Zambia apassengerservicechargeat such rates as theCompanymay determine.
Thechargeat designated airports shall be a debt due to theCompanyand shall be paid to theCompanyprior to em-barkation.
[As amended by No. 1 of 1973, No. 2 of 1976, No. 5 of 1978, No. 3 of 1980, No. 3 of 1985, No. 5 of 1987, No. 12 of 1989 and No. 16 of 1990]

Subject to the provisions of section four, there shall be paid by everypassengeron every occasion on which he embarks on anaircraftother than adesignated airportat anyairportfor a destination outside Zambia, apassengerservicechargeat such rate as the Minister may by statutory instrument determine:
Provided that apassengerfor a destination outside Zambia who is a national or resident of Zambia may pay the equivalent of thechargein kwacha.
Thechargeat anairport, other than adesignated airport, shall be a debt due to the Government and shall be paid to thecollection agentprior to embarkation.

Subject to section four there shall be paid by everypassengerembarking on anaircraftat adesignated airportfor a destination outside or within Zambia apassengerservicechargeat such rates as theCompanymay determine.

Thechargeat designated airports shall be a debt due to theCompanyand shall be paid to theCompanyprior to em-barkation.
[As amended by No. 1 of 1973, No. 2 of 1976, No. 5 of 1978, No. 3 of 1980, No. 3 of 1985, No. 5 of 1987, No. 12 of 1989 and No. 16 of 1990]
the head of any mission of any Commonwealth country and such members of his staff as are entitled to diplomatic privileges;
members of the diplomatic or permanent consular service of any foreign country;
such members of the staff of any international organisation recognised as such under the laws of the Republic as are entitled to diplomatic privileges;
any child under the age of two years;
anypassengerembarking at anairportat which he is in transit;
such other class or classes of persons as the Minister may, by statutory instrument, approve.

Where anyaircrafthaving taken off from anairportreturns to the same or any otherairportwithout completing its journey for reasons of bad weather, engine trouble or any other like cause, nochargeshall be payable by anypassengerwho returns on board thataircraftand re-embarks on that or any otheraircraftfor resuming his discontinued journey.

For the purposes of subsection (1), apassengeris in transit at anairportif—
he disembarks at suchairportfrom oneaircraftand re-embarks there on the same or anotheraircraft; and
between so disembarking and re-embarking he does not leave theairport.
he disembarks at suchairportfrom oneaircraftand re-embarks there on the same or anotheraircraft; and
between so disembarking and re-embarking he does not leave theairport.

Thecollection agentshall collect thechargefrom all persons liable to pay it.
Where thecollection agentfails or neglects to collect thechargefrom any person liable to pay thecharge, thecollection agentitself shall be liable for thechargein respect of that person.
Thecollection agentshall be paid commission on collection of thechargeat such rate as may be determined by the Minister.
Thecollection agentshall, within thirty days of the last day of each calendar month—
pay to thePermanent Secretarythe amounts collected by way ofcharge, together with the amount for which thecollection agentis liable under subsection (2) less the amount of commission due to it; and
submit to thePermanent Secretarya return in such form as may be prescribed by regulations.
ThePermanent Secretaryor any public officer authorised by him under his hand may recover from thecollection agentany amount due to the Government under the provisions of this section, together with costs of suit, in a subordinate court presided over by a senior resident magistrate.
A subordinate court presided over by a senior resident magistrate shall have the jurisdiction to hear and determine any suit under the provisions of subsection (5), notwithstanding that the value of the subject-matter of such suit exceeds the limit prescribed in relation to such court under the provisions of the Subordinate Courts Act or any other written law.
[Cap. 28]
All proceeds of thechargereceived or recovered by thePermanent Secretaryor a public officer authorised by him shall be paid into the general revenues of the Republic.
[As amended by ActNo. 5 of 1978]
Thecollection agentshall collect thechargefrom all persons liable to pay it.
Where thecollection agentfails or neglects to collect thechargefrom any person liable to pay thecharge, thecollection agentitself shall be liable for thechargein respect of that person.
Thecollection agentshall be paid commission on collection of thechargeat such rate as may be determined by the Minister.

Thecollection agentshall, within thirty days of the last day of each calendar month—
pay to thePermanent Secretarythe amounts collected by way ofcharge, together with the amount for which thecollection agentis liable under subsection (2) less the amount of commission due to it; and
submit to thePermanent Secretarya return in such form as may be prescribed by regulations.
pay to thePermanent Secretarythe amounts collected by way ofcharge, together with the amount for which thecollection agentis liable under subsection (2) less the amount of commission due to it; and
submit to thePermanent Secretarya return in such form as may be prescribed by regulations.

ThePermanent Secretaryor any public officer authorised by him under his hand may recover from thecollection agentany amount due to the Government under the provisions of this section, together with costs of suit, in a subordinate court presided over by a senior resident magistrate.

A subordinate court presided over by a senior resident magistrate shall have the jurisdiction to hear and determine any suit under the provisions of subsection (5), notwithstanding that the value of the subject-matter of such suit exceeds the limit prescribed in relation to such court under the provisions of the Subordinate Courts Act or any other written law.
[Cap. 28]

All proceeds of thechargereceived or recovered by thePermanent Secretaryor a public officer authorised by him shall be paid into the general revenues of the Republic.
[As amended by ActNo. 5 of 1978]

Thecollection agentand every servant employed by it who collects thechargeshall issue a receipt in the prescribed form to the person from whom thechargeis collected.
Thecollection agentand any servant of thecollection agentwho collects thechargebut who intentionally omits to issue a receipt as required by subsection (1) or in any manner delays or defers the issue thereof, shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding two thousand penalty units or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding three months, or to both; and in any prosecution for an offence under this subsection, proof of the commission of any of the acts charged shall beprima facieevidence of the intent of the accused to commit such act.
[As amended by ActNo. 13 of 1994]
Thecollection agentand every servant employed by it who collects thechargeshall issue a receipt in the prescribed form to the person from whom thechargeis collected.

Thecollection agentand any servant of thecollection agentwho collects thechargebut who intentionally omits to issue a receipt as required by subsection (1) or in any manner delays or defers the issue thereof, shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding two thousand penalty units or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding three months, or to both; and in any prosecution for an offence under this subsection, proof of the commission of any of the acts charged shall beprima facieevidence of the intent of the accused to commit such act.
[As amended by ActNo. 13 of 1994]

if such person has embarked on anaircraft, require him to leave theaircraft, and, if such person fails to do so within a reasonable time, may remove him from theaircraft;
